title: Laakteen Depot
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Structure |image=[[File:LaakteenDepot-CSW.png]] |name=Laakteen Depot |constructed=By c. [[53 BBY/Legends|53 BBY]]''[[Maul: Lockdown]]'' |destroyed=[[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] {{C|[[Great ReSynchronization|35:9]]}}The [[destruction of Laakteen Depot]] coincides with the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor''{{'s}}]] maiden voyage, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], or [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]]. This date corresponds to 35:9 in the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, according to ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. |type=Outpost"[[It's a Trap]]" |location=[[Laakteen/Legends|Laakteen]]{{FactFile|19|VAD 7-12|Darth Vader}}{{FigurineCite|1|Spaceships and Vehicles}} |interest= |rebuilt= |builder= |height= |width= |affiliation=*[[Pirate/Legends|Pirates]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]{{CSWECite|II|239|Laakteen Depot}} }} {{Quote|Our ''defense shield'' is shattered! We never hada ''chance!'' Warn ''Yavin Base…WARN YAVIN!''|Laakteen Depot is destroyed|}} '''Laakteen Depot''', located just off the [[Giju Run/Legends|Giju Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region, was a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] base and later the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] closest outpost to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] early during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Rebellion fought nearly a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] to win Laakteen Depot, capturing the base through the efforts of ace [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] [[Kufra Ahara]] and the [[Sanjin Green Hawks]]. Six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the loss of the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] [[Destruction of Laakteen Depot|destroyed]] Laakteen Depot during the ship's maiden voyage as a deliberate show of Imperial [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] might. By annihilating the Rebel base with his newly completed [[Warship/Legends|warship]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] intended to demonstrate to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] the Empire's supremacy. ==Description== Laakteen Depot was an outpost located on the [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] known as [[Laakteen/Legends|Laakteen]], which sat near the [[Giju Run/Legends|Giju Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' The outpost consisted of a series of interconnected domed structures. By the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], while controlled by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Laakteen Depot housed a [[Deflector shield/Legends|defense shield]] and at least one large [[Cannon/Legends|gun emplacement]]. ==History== ===Republic era=== {{Quote|What did you do for Iram Radique?''"
"''Saved his life.''"
"''Radique's? When was this?''"
"''Twenty years ago. On Lakteen Depot [sic]. Near the Giju Run.|[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] and Artagan Truax|}} Laakteen Depot was constructed as early as circa [[53 BBY/Legends|53 BBY]], some twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] ascension to [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], Laakteen Depot played host to an incident in which the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]], a criminal cult of [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-worshipers,{{CSWECite|I|57|Bando Gora}} planned to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Iram Radique]]. One of the cultists, [[Artagan Truax]], betrayed the Bando Gora and rescued Radique from them. Radique later established himself as a clandestine weapons dealer within the [[Cog Hive Seven]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]], where he protected Truax from the Bando Gora's wrath. ===Galactic Civil War=== {{Quote|There's no ''mistake?'' We fought nearly a ''year'' to win Laakteen Depot!''"
"''As of now, it doesn't ''exist!''|Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and General [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]]|}} By the beginnings of the Galactic Civil War between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the Rebel Alliance, Laakteen Depot served as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] base. At some point before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Rebellion captured Laakteen Depot after nearly a year of fighting. Modifications performed by [[Murachaun]] ace [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] [[Kufra Ahara]] helped the [[Sanjin Green Hawks]] to take the outpost, which became the Rebellion's closest [[Alliance Military/Legends|military]] base to the planet [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], home to one of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] foremost [[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|shipyards]]. [[File:LaakteenExecutor-CSW.png|thumb|left|275px|Darth Vader christened the ''Executor'' with the destruction of the Rebel base at Laakteen Depot.]] In [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]], six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], the Fondor Shipyards completed construction of the Empire's new [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] and the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. On the [[Warship/Legends|warship's]] maiden voyage,{{CSWECite|I|245|''Executor''}} Vader formally christened the ''Executor'' by [[Destruction of Laakteen Depot|annihilating]] the Rebellion's nearest installation, Laakteen Depot, in a tremendous show of force. Laakteen Depot's destruction was both [[Politics/Legends|political]] and military in nature. Vader intended to demonstrate to the Rebels and the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] that the Alliance's victory over the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] at the Battle of Yavin was pure chance and that the ''Executor'' was unstoppable. In their final moments, as the ''Executor''{{'s}} attack shattered the base's defense shield, the Rebels of Laakteen Depot sent a frantic warning to the main [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] that Vader's new warship was closing in, forcing the Rebellion to scramble to [[Evacuation of Yavin|evacuate]] its Yavin headquarters. ==Behind the scenes== Laakteen Depot first appeared in the ''[[It's a Trap]]'' comic strip, written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]]. ''It's a Trap'' was originally published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] as a newspaper [[Star Wars (comic strip)|serial comic]]{{InsiderCite|112|The Empire Strips Back}} from [[November]] [[1982]] to [[January]] [[1983]]. Its stories told the events that took place between the films [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].''[[Classic Star Wars Volume 2: The Rebel Storm]]'', "Introduction" In the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], Laakteen Depot serves to introduce the operational status and might of the ''Executor'', [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader's newly completed Super [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. Several [[Canon|continuity]] errors regarding Laakteen Depot arose in subsequent source material.{{FactFile|47|EXE 1-2|Executor}} The [[2000]] ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' mistakenly states that Laakteen Depot was a Rebel Alliance outpost around the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{C|"Laakteen Depot"}} while the [[2005]] [[Star Wars: The Official Figurine Collection 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Official Figurine Collection]]'' magazine states that Darth Vader destroyed Laakteen Depot prior to the Battle of Yavin. Additionally, [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 47|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 47]] credits the ''Executor''{{'s}} maiden voyage and destruction of Laakteen Depot to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Amise Griff]], an Imperial character who figures prominently in the storyline"[[Race for Survival]]" but who does not command the ''Executor'' during its maiden voyage in the ''It's a Trap'' comic strip. Finally, both the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and the [[2014]] novel ''[[Maul: Lockdown]]'' misspell the subject as "Lakteen." ==Appearances== *''[[Maul: Lockdown]]'' {{Ab|Maul: Lockdown (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} {{ID|Lakteen Depot}} *"[[It's a Trap]]" {{Reprint|It's a Trap}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{FactFile|19|VAD 7-12|Darth Vader}} *{{FactFile|47|EXE 1-2|Executor}} *''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' *{{FigurineCite|1|Spaceships and Vehicles}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{DB|starship|superstardestroyer|Super Star Destroyer}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Deposito Laakteen |da=Laakteen Depot |de=Laakteen-Depot-System }} [[Category:Alliance to Restore the Republic military bases]] [[Category:Pirate bases]]
